task_name: Post-operative Care Guidelines after Laparoscopic Surgery
reason_not_scored: This is an educational/instructional video with narration, overlays, and lecture content. It is not a performance video of an FLS task (peg transfer, pattern cut, endoloop, extracorporeal suture, or intracorporeal suture). The content covers post-operative dos and don'ts, lifestyle modifications, and recovery expectations.
video_type_indicators: Embedded video clips with overlay information
Educational graphics (hospital building, laparoscopic instruments, surgical team)
No timed procedural task performance visible
Content includes diet recommendations, activity restrictions, incision care, medication guidance, and recovery timeline

verbal_cues: Gently clean the incision site with mild soap and water and pat it dry
This helps prevent blood clots and promotes healing
Choose loose-fitting clothing that won't put pressure on the incision area
Maintain a healthy diet rich in fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins
Avoid lifting heavy objects or engaging in strenuous activities until your surgeon approves
Lifting heavy objects can strain the abdominal muscles and increase the risk of hernia or other complications
Attend follow-up appointments: Schedule and attend all post-operative appointments with your surgeon
Report any abnormal symptoms or concerns to your surgeon at World Laparoscopy Hospital promptly
Avoid picking at or scratching the incision site
Alcohol and smoking can impair the healing process and increase the risk of complications
The healing time after laparoscopic surgery varies, but typically the initial recovery period is shorter
By 4-6 weeks, incisions are typically well-healed, energy levels improve, and normal activities can be resumed
However, complete healing may take several months depending on the surgery and individual factors
common_errors_addressed: Premature heavy lifting and strenuous activity — video emphasizes restrictions to prevent hernia and complications
Smoking and alcohol use post-operatively — explicitly warned as impairing healing
Neglecting follow-up appointments — emphasized as critical for monitoring recovery
Picking or scratching incision site — cautioned to avoid infection and delayed healing
Discontinuing medications without surgeon consultation — flagged as potentially harmful
Ignoring warning symptoms — patient directed to report abnormal symptoms promptly

frame_analysis_summary: Video consists entirely of educational content: presenter delivering guidelines on post-operative care with supporting visual aids (embedded surgery footage, hospital facilities, instructional graphics). No FLS task performance is present.
scoring_determination: NOT APPLICABLE — This is instructional content, not a performance assessment. Per assessment guidelines, instructional videos are not scored for task completion; only teaching content is extracted.
